bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages, I am targeting maximum utility and have no reason to concede yet. Since all issues prefer valueC and the most important issueF also strongly favors valueC, the opening bid should anchor at my top outcome across every valid issue.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s only offer was all valueA, which is very poor for me and shows no concession pattern yet, so I will only make a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding my important issues at valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 1.06 by conceding only on a low-weight issue while keeping the more important issues at valueC. Since it is still very early and the opponent has repeated the all-valueA bid without concession, there is no reason to make a major move yet; I will avoid repeating my last exact bid by shifting the small concession from issueA to issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held at all valueA, with only a small move on issueD to valueB, so I will concede only on my lowest-weight issues while protecting high-weight issues C, D, E, and especially F.

I am targeting a high-utility early-game bid, conceding only on the low-weight issueA while preserving my strongest preferences on issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD. The opponent has mostly held at valueA for all issues, with only small movement on issueD or issueF, so there is not yet enough evidence to justify concessions on my high-weight issues at t=0.08.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.98 on my weighted scale by keeping valueC on the important issues C, D, E, and especially F. Since it is still early at t=0.10, I am only conceding on low-weight issues A and B; the opponent’s offers mostly hold at valueA with only small isolated concessions, and there are no messages to weigh.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.94 because it is early in the negotiation, so I should not concede on high-weight issues yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, though they have shown small concessions on individual higher-weight issues; since they consistently set issueA and issueB to valueA, I concede those low-weight issues first while holding issueC–issueF at valueC.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because the negotiation is early at t=0.14, but the opponent has consistently pushed toward valueA while recently showing some movement to valueB on issueC and issueD. I will keep my strongest positions on the most important issueF and other high-value issues, while conceding issueC to valueB to test whether their latest concessions indicate room for agreement.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.86 since it is still early at t=0.16, so I should not concede heavily yet. The opponent has mostly held at valueA but recently signaled willingness to give issueD=valueC and issueE=valueB, so I will build around those signals while holding firm on the most important issueF and other high-value issues.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.86, since time is still only 0.18 and the opponent has mostly held very low-value offers for me with only isolated concessions. Their recent offers signal possible flexibility on issueC, issueD, and issueE, so I concede the low-weight issueA/issueB and one mid-weight issueD to valueB while holding firm on issueF and the other important issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 because it is early in the negotiation, but I am making a measurable concession to avoid stalling. The opponent’s offers remain low for me, though their latest bid signaled some willingness on issueA and issueE; I will hold firm on the highest-weight issueF and most high-weight issues while conceding issueD and issueB.

At t=0.22 I am still targeting a high-utility bid, keeping my most important issueF at valueC while conceding the low-weight issues A and B to valueA. The opponent has repeatedly anchored on valueA but has recently offered valueC on issueD and issueE, and earlier valueC on issueC, so I am packaging those concessions back while avoiding a large early concession on issueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid around 0.86, since the negotiation is only at t=0.24 and there is no need to concede too deeply yet. The opponent has mostly held low values for A/B/C/D but their latest offer conceded issueE and issueF to valueC, so I keep those high-value issues firm while making concessions on low-weight A/B and a moderate concession on D. There are no messages to weigh, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.90 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.26, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall but show occasional concessions on issueB, issueC, issueE, and issueF; I will concede mainly on lower-weight issueA and partially on issueB/D while holding stronger positions on issueC, issueE, and especially issueF.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 because the negotiation is only at t=0.28, so there is no need to concede on my most important issues yet. The opponent has made limited concessions and their latest offer gives valueB on issueA, issueC, and issueD, so I am matching them on the lower-impact issueA and partially on issueD while holding firm on issueC, issueE, and especially issueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the high 0.8s by conceding mainly on lower-weight issues A and B and partially on D, while protecting the high-weight issueF and other important issues C/E. At t=0.30 there is some room to concede but no need to drop close to the opponent’s low offers yet; their bids have improved somewhat, including occasional valueC/valueB on C/D/E/F, but they remain far below my preferred region overall.

At t=0.32 there is still time, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making meaningful concessions on low-weight issues A and B and partially on C. The opponent’s latest offer concedes strongly on D and F, and they have previously offered E=valueC, so I am holding valueC on the most important issues F, E, and D while offering valueB on C as a moderate tradeoff.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility around 0.825, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues and one mid-weight issue while holding firm on issueF and issueE. At t=0.34 there is still time, but the opponent has mostly offered low-value bids to me with occasional concessions, so I am making a moderate counter that incorporates their latest signals on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD without giving up my highest-priority issueF.

I am targeting a moderate concession around the mid-game while preserving high utility on the most important issue, issueF, where the opponent has now offered valueC. The opponent’s latest bid shows movement on issueF and issueC but still asks for low values on issueD and issueE, so I concede issueD while holding issueE at valueC to keep the bid above reservation and avoid repeating my previous exact offer.

I am targeting a high but more conciliatory utility around 0.90, since we are still before the midpoint and there is no need to fall to the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent has begun conceding on important issues, especially issueD and issueF, and has occasionally offered valueB/valueC on other issues, so I am holding firm on high-weight issueE/issueF/issueD while conceding some lower-weight and mid-weight ground.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a strong but more realistic utility by holding firm on my most important issueF and keeping issueD high, while conceding lower-weight issueA and using compromises on issueC/issueE. The opponent’s offers have become somewhat better for me over time, including willingness to offer valueC on issueB/issueD and valueB on issueE, so this bid builds around those signals without dropping to their current offer. There are no natural-language messages to factor in.

At t=0.42 we are in the mid-game, so I am still targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions on lower-weight issues and incorporating values the opponent has recently signaled. The opponent’s latest valid offer gave valueC on issueA and issueE but remained poor for my most important issueF, so I hold issueF at valueC while offering issueB: valueA and issueC: valueB as tradeoffs.

I am targeting a high but slightly softened utility around the mid-game, keeping issueF at valueC because it is my most important issue while using concessions on lower-weight issueB and issueA. The opponent’s latest valid offer improved substantially on issueC and issueD for me, so I am matching those while not yet conceding the high-value issueE/issueF combination this far from the deadline.

At t=0.46 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.86, conceding on lower-weight issueA and moderately on issueC/issueE while holding the most important issueF and issueD at valueC. The opponent has moved away from all-valueA over time, especially showing willingness on issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueF, with no natural-language messages to alter that read.

I am targeting a strong mid-game utility around 0.825 by accepting much of the opponent’s latest movement while holding firm on issueF, my most important issue. At t=0.48 there is time left, so I should not fully concede to their last offer yet; their recent bids show willingness to move to valueB/valueC on several issues, including issueF in prior rounds, with no natural-language constraints to credit.

At mid-game (t=0.50), I am targeting a solid but more conciliatory bid around the high-0.7 utility range for me, while still protecting issueF, my most important issue. The opponent has begun offering valueC on issueF and occasionally on other issues, so I keep issueF at valueC and concede more on lower/medium-weight issues where they often push toward valueA. With no messages, I rely only on the offer pattern and avoid repeating my previous bid.

I am targeting the opponent's most recent offer because it gives me a strong utility around 0.825 while preserving my highest-weight issues E and F at valueC. Since we are just past the midpoint, the opponent has clearly conceded from all valueA toward B/C on several issues, so matching their last valid bid is the safest way to lock in a good agreement rather than risk unnecessary delay.
